records.ety: [OF. porte coulisse, colece, a sliding door, fr. L. colare, colatum, to filter, to strain: cf. F. couler to glide. See Port a gate, and cf. Cullis, Colander.]

defs: A grating of iron or of timbers pointed with iron, hung over the gateway of a fortress, to be let down to prevent the entrance of an enemy.
quotes: She . . . the huge portcullis high updrew.

defs: An English coin of the reign of Elizabeth, struck for the use of the East India Company; -- so called from its bearing the figure of a portcullis on the reverse.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Portcullised (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Portcullising.]

defs: To obstruct with, or as with, a portcullis; to shut; to bar.
